In Re: Recruitment of Visually Impaired in Judicial Services
(iii) To issue a Mandamus directing the Respondent No.1 to 
hold the separate main examinations for the candidates 
of persons with benchmark disabilities category including 
the petitioner herein, for selection in the Rajasthan Judicial 
Service Examination, 2024;
(iv) To issue a Mandamus directing the Respondent No.1 
to declare the results for the candidates of persons with 
benchmark disabilities category separately for each stage 
of shortlisting for the purposes of selection in the Rajasthan 
Judicial Service Examination, 2024. 
III. 
SUMMARY OF PLEADINGS & SUBMISSIONS OF LEARNED 
COUNSELS
A. 
IN RE: RECRUITMENT OF VISUALLY IMPAIRED IN JUDICIAL 
SERVICES VS. REGISTRAR GENERAL, HIGH COURT OF 
MADHYA PRADESH AND OTHERS [SUO MOTU WRIT 
PETITION (CIVIL) NO.2 OF 2024] 
9. 
Based on the letter petition dated 15.01.2024 given by the mother 
of a visually impaired candidate to the Hon’ble Chief Justice of 
India, challenging the amendment made in Rule 6A of the Madhya 
Pradesh Judicial Service (Recruitment and Conditions of Service) 
Rules, 1994, whereby reservation granted in favour of blind and 
low vision persons, was withdrawn, this Court has registered the 
captioned suo motu Writ Petition. 
10. The respondents in this suo motu writ petition are the High Court 
of Madhya Pradesh, Government of Madhya Pradesh and Union of 
India. Vide order dated 15.04.2024, this Court impleaded one Dr. 
Sanjay S. Jain, a professor of Law at the National Law School of 
India University, Bangalore, with over 25 years of teaching experience 
and totally blind since birth, as an intervenor, to assist the Court in 
connection with the present issue concerning the appointment of 
visually impaired persons as Judges in District Judiciary. 
Submissions of the Letter Petitioner
